raw_scrape_rows.content_excerpt:
Commercial Linkage Fees (CLF) are a standard tool used by local communities for generating affordable housing resources. The CLF is similar to other impact fees levied on new development, and helps cover the cost associated with creating new or expanded public facilities to meet the additional demand created by the development, such as parks, schools, libraries and streets. Before levying an impact fee, jurisdictions are required by state law to complete a nexus study that shows the linkage between the new development and the increased demand for the facilities. The results of the nexus study establish the maximum legal fee that may be charged.

In the case of the CLF, as cities build jobs-g

selected_chunks.snippet: On Tuesday, September 1st, 2020, the San Jose City Council approved a Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) Ordinance, joining all of the other major cities in the South Bay that have already adopted fees. CLF

Commercial Linkage Fees (CLF) are a standard tool used by local communities for generating affordable housing resources. The CLF is similar to other impact fees levied on new development, and helps co

Following the nexus study, local city councils set fees at levels expected to generate resources for local affordable housing development without making commercial development infeasible. In some juri

In the case of the CLF, as cities build jobs-generating commercial real estate – like new office buildings, retail space, or warehouses – commercial developers pay a fee to offset a small portion of t

The ordinance will be applied retroactively to all projects approved after September, 2019. The fee level will be set at the rate that is in place when developers receive their building permits, but f
#### Commercial Linkage Fee in San Jose
While this was most definitely a win for the hundreds of people in the city who have advocated for this measure over the last few years, the adopted fees are the lowest in the county and exclude some
- City of Santa Clara 2017 Affordable Housing Requirements Ordinance
SV@Home is working to raise additional funds for housing for working families by achieving full participation from all cities and the County to adopt Commercial Linkage Fees.
Key components of a successful commercial linkage fee ordinance include:
- Keyser Marston Associates: Summary of Jobs Housing Linkage Fee Programs – California (2018)
- City of Mountain View 2013 Commercial Linkage Fee Ordinance
- City of Sunnyvale 2015 Commercial Linkage Fee Ordinance
- The fee must be balanced so it will generate funding sufficient to develop new affordable homes for new employees, but not be set so high that it makes commercial development infeasible.
#### SV@Home’s Position: We Need New Local Sources of Revenue for Housing, Especially As We Add New Jobs
- All job-producing uses should pay a fee, although fee levels should be feasible for each development type
- It is often important to consider the size of developments in establishing fee structures, as the fees may have a greater impact on smaller developments
- City of San Jose July 2020 Feasibility Study
- City of San Jose July 2020 Nexus Study
- Grounded Solutions Network: Commercial Linkage Fees Webpage

top_results.snippet: The city’s consultant, Keyser Marston Associates, found that a fee of up to $176 per square foot for new retail buildings would “reflect the cost of mitigating affordable housing impacts of new development.” The consultant identified $151 per square foot as the reflective cost of new high-tech office buildings. “Our business is already a tenuous situation… I don’t think that this is the time to increase fees for commercial (developers) and job creators in the city of San Jose.”. “Our business is already a tenuous situation… I don’t think that this is the time to increase fees for commercial (developers) and job creators in the city of San Jose.”. Literally, other cities have millions from residential AND commercial fees – and San Jose has already lost out on tens of millions that could actually make more Measure A projects viable…or more affordable housing generally.
